summary: Frontend testing of Streamlit 'Mapa dos Contentores' page after streamlit-elements implementation. CRITICAL BUG PERSISTS: Drag-and-drop position persistence still NOT working due to streamlit-elements sync() callback incompatibility with Streamlit 1.53+

ui_bugs.issue: Position persistence FAILS - the sync() callback from streamlit-elements is NOT syncing layout changes to session_state. Visual drag works but clicking 'Guardar layout' shows 'Nenhuma alteração de posição para guardar' even after dragging

integration_issues.flow: Contentor drag -> session_state sync -> Guardar layout -> DB persistence
integration_issues.issue: streamlit-elements onLayoutChange=sync('layout_contentores_mapa') callback is broken in Streamlit 1.53.1. The callback does not fire or update session_state. This is a known library incompatibility issue since Streamlit 1.34+

issues: No block duplication - PASS (2 contentores shown correctly)
No iframe sandbox SecurityError - PASS (previous bug fixed)

action_items: CRITICAL: The streamlit-elements library (v0.1.0) has broken callbacks since Streamlit 1.34+. The sync() function does NOT update session_state when onLayoutChange fires.
Option 1: DOWNGRADE Streamlit to <1.34 (e.g., 1.32.x) where streamlit-elements worked correctly
Option 2: Implement manual persistence using st.components.v1.html with postMessage API to communicate position changes to parent
Option 3: Use a different draggable grid library like streamlit-aggrid or implement custom native Streamlit solution
Option 4: Fork streamlit-elements and fix the callback issue for newer Streamlit versions

APPLIED FIX (did not work): Modified /root/.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/streamlit_elements/core/callback.py to change import from 'components' to 'custom_component as components' - this alone was insufficient
critical_code_review_comments: Line 1699 in app.py: onLayoutChange=sync('layout_contentores_mapa') - this callback is not firing due to library incompatibility
The streamlit-elements library is no longer maintained (last update was 2023) and has known issues with Streamlit 1.34+
Current Streamlit version is 1.53.1 which is incompatible with streamlit-elements callbacks

success_rate.frontend: 70% - visual features work, persistence does not

seed_data_creation: CT-TEMP (110 palhetas), CT-TEST-001 (0 palhetas) contentores exist
context_for_next_testing_agent: The drag-and-drop visual is working. The streamlit-elements Grid renders correctly inside iframe. The issue is sync() callback not updating session_state. Verified with Playwright: dragged item moves visually from x=556 to x=699, but session_state.layout_contentores_mapa is NOT updated, causing 'Guardar layout' to report 'Nenhuma alteração'. Main agent needs to either downgrade Streamlit or use alternative approach.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: streamlit-elements library (v0.1.0) callback mechanism is incompatible with Streamlit 1.34+ due to changes in how Streamlit handles custom component callbacks. The sync() function relies on internal Streamlit APIs that changed.
rca_of_the_issue.error_behavior: No explicit error - the drag works visually but onLayoutChange callback silently fails to update session_state
reproduction_steps: 3. Drag CT-TEMP contentor to a new position
6. See message: 'Nenhuma alteração de posição para guardar'
7. Reload page - position is back to original
mitigation_options: Use different library (streamlit-aggrid, react-grid-layout via st.components)
Implement manual callback using postMessage/custom component

features_tested.mapa_contentores_visual: PASS - Professional design, grid layout, no duplication
features_tested.drag_and_drop_visual: PASS - Contentors can be dragged visually in iframe
features_tested.drag_and_drop_persistence: FAIL - Position NOT persisted due to sync() callback failure
features_tested.no_iframe_sandbox_error: PASS - Previous SecurityError is FIXED (no allow-top-navigation error)
